signals.headline: Germany Inflation Hits 2-Year High at 2
signals.raw:
Germany Inflation Hits 2-Year High at 2.7% in March | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's annual inflation rate reached 2.7% in March 2026, the highest since January 2024, driven by energy prices rising for the first time since 2023, as confirmed by the Federal Statistical Office (Destatis).[3] This exceeds prior forecasts like OECD's 2.9% projection and could shift prediction markets on Eurozone

signals.headline: South Korea GDP Growth Forecast Slashed to 1
signals.raw:
South Korea GDP Growth Forecast Slashed to 1.7% by OECD | YES | MED confidence**  
OECD lowered South Korea's 2026 GDP growth forecast to 1.7% from 2.1%, citing Middle East conflict risks and energy import dependence, raising stagflation concerns.[1] Supplementary 25 trillion won budget announced but effectiveness doubted; private forecasts like Natexis at 1% add bearish signals for growth markets

signals.headline: South Korea Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Inflation Rises to 2.2% in March, Below Forecasts | NO | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% prior, but missed market expectations of 2.4%, per Statistics Korea data, signaling contained pressures amid energy volatility.[2] This aligns with Bank of Korea's 2% target and may ease markets betting on rate hikes.[2][4]

signals.headline: Global Annual Inflation Forecast Upped to 4% for G20 by OECD | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Global Annual Inflation Forecast Upped to 4% for G20 by OECD | YES | MED confidence**  
OECD's interim outlook raised G20 consumer inflation to 4% for 2026 (up 1.2 points), with South Korea at 2.7% and Germany at 2.9%, due to Middle East-driven energy surges.[1] J.P. Morgan sees stable global core CPI at 2.8%, but regional upticks could move broader inflation bets.[1][4]
